A simple phone call service hailed as saving the lives of thousands of arrested Aboriginal people is under threat because of a funding feud between the state and federal governments. arbiter.why: The claim incorrectly attributes the reallocation to the English runner-up berth, whereas the source only discusses the UEFA Champions League holders' berth. text_claims.text: The Carteret ministry was the ministry that was in office between 1742 and 1744 following the defeat of Sir Robert Walpole's ministry by a margin of 1 vote.

evidence.sentence: At this time the King was still the effective head of the Government; ministers regarded and referred to him as their 'Master' and themselves as his 'servants'; the 'Closet', where the most important of them worked with him, was a higher political level than the Cabinet; the Government of the day and their supporters were known as the 'Court'; and the symbol and sign of real ministerial power was 'habitual, frequent, familiar access' to the King.
evidence.snippet: At this time the King was still
evidence.reason: The passage discusses Sir Robert Walpole's ministry but does not mention the Carteret ministry or its dates, nor does it mention Walpole's defeat by a margin of one vote.
evidence.window: Walpole's premiership inaugurated a new type of prime minister, seated in the House of Commons, as first lord of the Treasury and chancellor of the Exchequer, with the dual function of 'minister with the King in the House of Commons', and 'minister for the House of Commons in the Closet'. At this time the King was still the effective head of the Government; ministers regarded and referred to him as their 'Master' and themselves as his 'servants'; the 'Closet', where the most important of them worked with him, was a higher political level than the Cabinet; the Government of the day and their supporters were known as the 'Court'; and the symbol and sign of real ministerial power was 'habitual, frequent, familiar access' to the King. By creating a minister who, in Pitt's words, 'went directly' between the King and the House of Commons, Walpole supplied a link, hitherto missing, between the two centres of political power.23 Walpole introduced this innovation by electing to remain in the House of Commons when, in 1721, he entered on his long period of office as head of the Treasury.
